title: 中考六选五 3: The Hidden Cost of Multitasking
passage:
Many students believe they can do several things at the same time. They may listen to music, reply to messages and do homework together. 1. ______ However, what feels efficient is not always truly efficient.

The human brain is not as good at multitasking as many people think. When students move from one task to another, their attention has to restart. 2. ______ This small loss of attention happens again and again, making the work slower.

Multitasking can also reduce the quality of learning. If students check their phones while reading, they may still finish the page. 3. ______ They may remember fewer details and misunderstand important ideas.

There is also an emotional cost. Constant switching can make the mind feel busy and restless. 4. ______ After a while, students may feel tired even if they have not done much serious work.

To study better, students should create blocks of focused time. For example, they can study for twenty-five minutes and then take a short break. 5. ______ This simple habit can make learning calmer and more effective.

questions.label: Questions 1-5 - Sentence Insertion
questions.instruction: Choose the best sentence from A-F for each blank in the passage. There is one extra sentence you do not need.

choices.text: But finishing a page does not mean understanding it well.
During that time, phones and unrelated tabs should be kept away.
This makes them feel as if they are saving time.
Their brains need extra time to return to the main task.
A quiet environment is useful, but it cannot solve every study problem.
The mind never gets enough time to settle deeply on one thing.
